last_verdict.tests: 2013 passed, 24 skipped (.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q). 2026-06-21: AUDIO PIPELINE OVERHAUL landed on main (e22935c) -- WebRTC APM echo-cancel backend (core/engines/_apm.py, aec_backend='apm', 41-53 dB ERLE in-vitro via livekit, fails open) + apm_always_on (NS/AGC/HPF every block, Teams-parity idle cleanup) + aec_auto_delay (wires the previously-unwired measured_delay_samples) + WASAPI capture_voice_comm; FLUID TTS now default (tts_target_rms=0.12 per-sentence loudness norm, barge_fade_ms=4 de-click on flush, tts_declick_threshold=0.22); committed open_speaker device profile; mobile declick+normalize port; doctor checks soxr/livekit/PipeWire-EC. Defaults safe (clean clone byte-identical except tts_target_rms). NEEDS LIVE MIC VALIDATION (barge_stress on --device open_speaker) + speaker re-enroll after enabling cleanup + flutter analyze on mobile. soxr+livekit installed in venv this session. See docs/session_2026-06-21_audio_apm_quickwins.md + docs/audio_pipeline.md + memory audio-quality-roadmap-2026-06-21. --- PRIOR (2026-06-20): 1958 passed, 25 skipped. FIVE waves + a LIVE test that session. Wave 5: LIVE-validated asr-tts-2 (async second pass ENGAGED, 0 underflows/exceptions/crashes/self-interrupts, barge-in cuts real talk-overs) BUT surfaced two problems: (1) the MIC IS CLIPPING 36-65% of blocks (peak=1.0, rms~0.5) -> OS mic gain too hot -> garbled STT + SenseVoice hallucinations ('I.'); OWNER must lower OS mic input level/disable mic-boost (input_gain=1.0 so it's not code). (2) owner CONFIRMED real acoustic OUTPUT artifacts (buzzy/glitchy TTS, not just garbled words) = the run-103630 white-noise still partly present; _audio_cb silently zero-fills FIFO underruns. ADDED DIAGNOSTICS (real-time-safe, reviewed SHIP): input-clipping warning in the capture heartbeat (clip=% + one-shot WARN >2%), output-underrun counter in _audio_cb (per-reply WARN + playback_underrun metric + heartbeat underruns=N). NEXT: owner lowers mic gain + re-runs -> clip% should ~0 (STT jumps) and underruns=N confirms whether the buzzy artifacts are FIFO underruns from CPU contention (async SenseVoice worker + TTS synth on the desktop CPU tier) -> if so, per-tier TTS/worker thread budgeting. Also: auto-detect picked 'desktop' not 'desktop_gpu_4090' (probe reads 16376MB < 16GB threshold -- recommend_profile off-by-rounding). --- Earlier waves 1-4: Wave 4 (low-value headless cleanup): llm-inference-9 (LlamaCppLLM type_k/type_v KV-cache quant plumbing; phone/phone_lite quantize the K cache to q8_0, V left at f16 since V-quant needs flash-attn; fail-soft on an old lib) + control-plane-2 (load-elastic admission: supervisor caps concurrency to 1 under sustained load via an ungated load_fraction reader threaded app->runtime->supervisor, separate from the live_routing-gated load_snapshot). DEFERRED with reasons: llm-inference-6 (per-call timeout blocked by the single-context lock; supervisor 25s reap backstops it), llm-inference-7 (Q3 weights config flip needs on-device quality validation). Reviewed (1 refuted, nits+1 LOW; LOW fixed -> K-only). --- Wave 3: Wave 3 (headless): control-plane-3 -- EWMA-scaled ADAPTIVE watchdog deadlines. The recorder now folds a TTS first-audio EWMA (recent_tts_ms) alongside the TTFT one; the watchdog's 'llm stuck'/'tts stuck' deadlines = clamp(4*recent_ewma, floor, ceil) with cold-start fallback to the static 10s/5s. LLM floor 6s (above a heavy-tier first-token, since the EWMA blends tiers), ceil 30s; ADAPTIVE_DEADLINES toggle pins legacy. Net: fast device surfaces a real stall sooner, slow phone stops false-flagging honest long turns (attacks the stuck:1 bundle noise). Diagnosis only -- supervisor task-reap is the real hang bound. Adversarially reviewed (0 refuted, nits+1 LOW, fixed). Scouted first: routing-cascade-6 already neutralized by LLMCapabilityRouter cache, routing-cascade-7 done, routing-cascade-4/asr-tts-3 need real models/audio. --- Wave 1+2: Wave 1: asr-tts-2 (async second-pass ASR) + asr-tts-1 (per-tier ASR policy). Wave 2 (headless, no live test): llm-inference-3 (LlamaCppLLM num_predict->max_tokens cap translation + phone/phone_lite caps) + rc-5 (SUPERSEDED metric so newest-input-wins-preempted turns don't false-flag 'llm stuck') + invariant hardening (no profile may disable speaker_gate_input / agent_brain.require_owner_verified). Both waves adversarially reviewed; wave-2 review CAUGHT A BLOCKER (a config _comment key leaking into llama.cpp's strict create_chat_completion signature) -> fixed by dropping _-prefixed option keys + a strict-signature regression test. Scouted Phase-1 first: device-adapt-1/cross-platform-8/cloud-invariant were already done.
last_verdict.current: 1999 passed, 24 skipped. 2026-06-20 (pt3 / TTS DE-CLICK + BARGE-IN P1 VALIDATION): ROOT-CAUSED the owner's 'strange noise'/buzzy TTS -> it is the shipped VITS voice (en_US-libritts_r-medium) emitting DETERMINISTIC sample-level IMPULSE SPIKES on certain text (dozens/sentence), reproducible in standalone synth with ZERO engine load. This OVERTURNS the prior FIFO-underrun/CPU-contention theory (the run's 5 underruns were benign barge-cut flushes; producer ran AHEAD). FIX SHIPPED (main e0cfb39): core.audio_frontend.declick (3-pt-median impulse repair on the synth/producer thread, never the real-time _audio_cb) wired into both _synthesize paths behind sherpa.tts_declick (default on). Measured impulse clicks 26->0 and 70->1, NO-OP on clean speech (corr 1.0000, 0 samples touched); tests/test_declick.py. A/B clips sent, owner confirmed by ear. Caveat: measure clicks as IMPULSES (jump-and-return), not raw |diff|>0.3 edges (those over-count loud-speech slopes). BARGE-IN P1 VALIDATED via the new tools.autotest.barge_stress (many self-interrupt + talk-over trials in one session): TALK-OVER CUT RATE 6/6 = 100%, 1 self-interrupt FP in 5 (fp_rate 0.20), ~3s cut latency -- on the BARE LAPTOP SPEAKER. OPEN: re-run with declick active to see if that 1 FP -> 0 (BLOCKED: JBL powered off mid-session, won't reconnect from software). STT CLARIFIED: OTA WER (0.3-0.9) is a DOUBLE-CAPTURE TEST ARTIFACT; fed DIRECTLY the recordings transcribe ~0.10 WER -> real on-device STT is GOOD; judge STT from direct/cable, not OTA. Latent follow-up: _audio_cb does an O(n) resample+RMS on the hard-RT thread when play_sr!=16k+AEC on -> move to producer if it bites. Handoff: docs/session_2026-06-20_tts_declick_and_bargein_validation.md. --- (pt2 / OTA TOPOLOGY FLIP 2026-06-19): owner flipped the OTA topology -> assistant on the BARE LAPTOP SPEAKER (the open-speaker P1 condition), user voice on the JBL via --inject-sink. ROOT-CAUSED the owner's 'recording sounds really bad': the mic's ANALOG ADC gain `amixer -c 1 sset Capture` defaults to +30dB and PipeWire RE-APPLIES it on every source suspend/resume -> the OTA capture CLIPS AT THE CONVERTER (peak 1.0, clip 16%) before any digital/source volume can help. Source recordings themselves are clean. WORKAROUND: a background loop re-asserting `amixer -c 1 sset Capture <~45-52%>` + `Internal Mic Boost 0` every 0.5s during the run (the gain-pinner). Also: the engine's PortAudio capture runs ~10x HOTTER than parecord/pacat at the same ADC gain -> only a real engine run is ground truth for peak/clip. TWO HARNESS FIXES LANDED + tests green (1992): (a) lead-in silence on injected clips -- audio.py inject(lead_in_ms=) + _with_lead_in() + acoustics inject_lead_in_ms (Speaker=500ms, Cable/Delay=0), threaded through voice_loop's 4 inject sites; THIS made S3 barge-in start firing (BT was dropping the talk-over onset). (b) JBL keep-alive -- SpeakerAcoustics.session() holds a Bluetooth inject_sink awake with a continuous silent `pacat /dev/zero` stream so each clip plays from a warm A2DP link (no cold-resume onset drop). RESULTS (subset, owner real voice): WER 0.94 (clipping) -> 0.34 (run5: keepalive+pinner, asst 55%/JBL 65%/ADC +3.75dB); individual clips 0.00 ('Paris') and 0.08 (sailor story); capital->'Central Perk' joke; rainbow refraction explained correctly. OPEN-SPEAKER BARGE-IN ON THE BARE LAPTOP SPEAKER: self-interrupt=0 (pass), barge-in=1 (pass) -- the P1 goal. AEC: aec_probe on the louder bundle -> BEST delay=40ms ERLE+5dB (laptop spk shares the chassis w/ the mic; the stale 260ms was the JBL/BT path) -> set aec_ref_delay_ms:40 in config.local.json (gitignored override). DEFERRED by owner: the JBL OUTPUT was too loud in the room -> owner powered it off -> full JBL OTA + level calibration is a follow-up. Handoff: docs/session_2026-06-19_ota_laptop_jbl_topology.md. --- (pt1 earlier 2026-06-19): autonomous harness RUN ON THE OWNER'S REAL RECORDED VOICE. Guided recorder (tools.autotest record) shipped + a record --check mic METER. BIG FIX: the built-in mic was clipping EVERYTHING (peak 1.0, 5-37% of samples) -> garbled STT. Root cause: built-in mic analog gain MAXED (Capture +30dB AND Internal Mic Boost +30dB = +60dB). The ALSA mixer can't be tamed (PipeWire manages/overrides it; parecord/pw-record IGNORE software volume) -- the knob that STICKS and that the sounddevice recorder HONORS is the PipeWire SOURCE VOLUME: `pactl set-source-volume @DEFAULT_SOURCE@ 40%` (persists). At 40% the owner records clean (clip 0%, rms ~0.07-0.14). Gotchas: the mic SOURCE SUSPENDS when idle -> one-shot sounddevice capture reads digital silence (hold awake with a throwaway `parecord ... &`; a full recording self-holds). CABLE RESULT on the owner's clean voice: mean WER 0.29 across 37 clips, 0 dropped, ALL 5 commands perfect, self-corrections UNDERSTOOD ('call John no James'->James; timer->10min; repeats collapsed). Needed a harness fix: CableAcoustics.inject_gain 100->55 (the engine captures the null-sink monitor ~2.7x hot -> unity injection clipped it -> ratcheted the echo/quiet floor -> dropped every clip after ~14 on LONG runs; short runs hid it). Remaining cable artifact: engine clips the FIRST PHONEME of each injected clip ('St an alarm','G me a recipe') -> add ~0.3s lead-in silence to injections -> expect WER ~0.18 (NOT done). OTA (over-the-air) WIRED but NOT yet working: added SpeakerAcoustics(inject_sink=) + --inject-sink so user clips play out a DIFFERENT speaker than the assistant (near/far). Owner added a JBL Flip 5 (bluez_output.D8_37_3B_19_CF_03.1, default sink = assistant output; A2DP output-only so laptop mic still captures). A 6-clip OTA subset FAILED (monitor_rms 0.059, STT garbage): mic at 40% + laptop speaker sink at 35% = user-clip playback too quiet at the mic. NEEDS LEVEL TUNING (raise laptop speaker + mic for OTA). OTA re-captures recordings through a speaker -> degraded STT (judge STT from CABLE, not OTA); OTA's value = self-interrupt/barge under the real JBL echo (JBL is BT -> ~150-250ms extra latency, so real ref delay >> configured 260ms; aec_probe the bundle). Owner asked to STOP live tuning + prepare setup for next session. Handoff: docs/session_2026-06-19_real_voice_ota_setup.md. recordings/ + logs/runs/ bundles hold the owner's REAL VOICE -> kept LOCAL ONLY (§9.7), not committed.

TOPOLOGY: assistant -> BARE LAPTOP SPEAKER (alsa_output.pci-0000_00_1f.3.analog-stereo, default sink); user voice -> JBL (bluez_output.D8_37_3B_19_CF_03.1) via --inject-sink; capture = laptop mic. STEPS: (1) power on + `bluetoothctl connect D8:37:3B:19:CF:03`; calibrate the JBL output level for a COMFORTABLE room that's still audible at the mic -- lever: room loudness (speaker vol) and mic pickup (ADC gain) are DECOUPLED, so keep speakers quiet + turn the MIC up, BUT too-quiet speakers drop user clips below the VAD speech-floor (run6 had empty transcripts). Sweet spot ~ run5 (asst ~55%, JBL ~65%, ADC ~+4dB) or quiet-room+higher-ADC tuned per JBL placement. (2) START THE ADC GAIN-PINNER before any OTA run: `while true; do amixer -c 1 sset Capture 50% >/dev/null; amixer -c 1 sset 'Internal Mic Boost' 0 >/dev/null; sleep 0.5; done &` (defeats PipeWire's reset-to-+30dB-on-resume that CLIPS the capture). (3) subset check: `.venv/bin/python -m tools.autotest voice --acoustics speaker --make-sound --inject-sink bluez_output.D8_37_3B_19_CF_03.1 --utterances recordings/owner_subset --llm ollama --model gemma3:4b` -> expect clean STT + barge-in PASS. (4) full OTA: same but --utterances recordings/owner. (5) aec_probe the bundle -> confirm aec_ref_delay_ms (currently 40 in config.local.json, measured for the laptop-speaker assistant). (6) STICKY ADC FIX: a WirePlumber/udev rule to cap the Capture ADC gain (replace the pinner hack).
